text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should start by making a sourdough starter. It's a bit involved, but it's totally worth it. Once you have your starter, you can start baking some loaves of bread. Now, while the bread is baking, you can sit and think about the vastness of the universe. It's actually mind-blowing when you think about it. Did you know that the universe is expanding every second? I just read that it's at an accelerating rate. Pretty wild, right? Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that crust. You want to make sure it's nice and crunchy, so you'll need to knead it for a while. It's a workout, for sure. Once the dough is shaped, you'll need to let it rise in a warm, dry place. While it's rising, you can watch a movie or read a book. I've been reading this book lately about history, and I just love it. It's so well-written. And to finish the loaf off properly, the last thing to do is to score the top with a sharp knife
